purpose.summary: This COBOL program deletes authorization details and summaries from an IMS database and takes checkpoints during the process. It handles IMS database operations for pending authorization records.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of a larger authorization processing system, responsible for cleaning up or purging pending authorization data.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending authorization detail segment to be deleted.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending authorization summary segment to be deleted.

inputs.name: PAUT-PCB-NUM
inputs.description: PCB number for the PAUT PCB.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the pending authorization detail segment.
Defines the structure of the pending authorization summary segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph deletes an authorization detail segment (PAUTDTL1) from the IMS database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the account ID if it is. It then executes a DLI DLET command to delete the segment using the PAUT-PCB-NUM. After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful. If successful (DIBSTAT is spaces), it increments WS-NO-DTL-DELETED. If the deletion fails, it displays an error message including the DIBSTAT and the account ID, and then calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph simply exits the 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL paragraph, returning control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph deletes an authorization summary segment (PAUTSUM0) from the IMS database. It begins by checking if debugging is enabled, and if so, displays the account ID. It then executes a DLI DLET command to delete the summary segment using the PAUT-PCB-NUM. Following the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to see if the deletion was successful. If successful (DIBSTAT is spaces), it increments WS-NO-SUMRY-DELETED. If the deletion fails, it displays an error message including the DIBSTAT and the account ID, and then calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph simply exits the 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph, returning control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph takes a checkpoint using the DLI CHKP command. It uses WK-CHKPT-ID as the checkpoint ID. After the checkpoint is taken, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the operation was successful. If successful (DIBSTAT is spaces), it increments WS-NO-CHKP. If WS-NO-CHKP reaches P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ, it resets WS-NO-CHKP to 0 and displays a checkpoint success message including the authorization count and the current application ID. If the checkpoint fails, it displays an error message including the DIBSTAT, record count, and application ID, and then calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph simply exits the 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT paragraph, returning control to the calling paragraph.
This paragraph is the program's abend routine. It displays a message indicating that the program is abending, sets the RETURN-CODE to 16, and then terminates the program using GOBACK.
This paragraph simply exits the 9999-ABEND paragraph, although it's unlikely to be reached in practice.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to SPACES in 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL
DIBSTAT not equal to SPACES in 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY
DIBSTAT not equal to SPACES in 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT

open_questions.suggestion: Review upstream JCL or scheduling systems.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of WK-CHKPT-ID?
open_questions.context: The purpose of the checkpoint ID is not clear from the code.
open_questions.suggestion: Review the program documentation or related code.
